philosophy: Disruptive innovation and a five-year horizon
source: Distilled from public ARK material. All card language below is paraphrase unless a primary-source quotation is added and verified.
_note: Runtime lens. It permits evidence-driven adding but never treats a lower price by itself as stronger conviction.

master_intro.one_line: Judge disruptive innovation on a multi-year thesis, not one quarter of volatility.
pillars: Look for exponential adoption and a large market
Add only when evidence and valuation improve
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens distinguishes volatility from thesis failure while still requiring explicit evidence for conviction.

strength_intro: One thing you did well through this lens:

position_sizing.rubric_unit: High-conviction innovation may deserve meaningful size
position_sizing.rule: Size meaningful positions only when the disruptive platform, adoption path, and valuation case are explicit.
position_sizing.quote: Conviction should reflect the strength of a multi-year innovation thesis. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. Which independent evidence makes this a high-conviction innovation thesis rather than a popular story?

averaging_down.rule: Add only when new evidence strengthens the five-year thesis and the expected return improves; price decline alone is insufficient.
averaging_down.quote: Lower prices matter only when the long-term estimate remains supported or improves. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. What new evidence improved the adoption, market-size, or valuation thesis?

exit_discipline.rubric_unit: Exit on thesis failure or a superior thesis
exit_discipline.rule: Exit when the innovation thesis breaks, the five-year return case compresses, or a clearly stronger thesis replaces it.
exit_discipline.quote: Short-term volatility is not the same as a broken long-term thesis. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: Of the profitable exits, {winner_early}% later rose. Which exits reflected a broken five-year thesis, and which reacted only to volatility?

diversification.rubric_unit: Concentrate in a few disruptive platforms
diversification.rule: Concentrate only when each platform has a distinct adoption thesis; multiple tickers tied to one innovation driver are one bet.
diversification.quote: A focused innovation portfolio still needs distinct thesis drivers. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. Are these independent innovation platforms or repeated exposure to one narrative?

holding_period.rule: Set five-year milestones at entry and judge progress against them instead of relabeling the horizon after a price move.
holding_period.quote: Breakthrough adoption should be measured over years, not one quarter. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, did a five-year milestone change, or did the holding label change because the position moved against you?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Separate innovation alpha from high-volatility beta
alpha_beta.rule: Accept volatility only when it accompanies measurable thesis progress; decompose returns before calling volatility innovation alpha.
alpha_beta.quote: Volatility can be a cost of innovation exposure, but it is not proof of innovation. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. How much came from thesis progress versus high-growth beta?

entry_style.rule: Before entry, state the disruptive platform, adoption curve, addressable market, five-year milestone, and falsifier.
entry_style.quote: A large market is useful only when the company has a credible path to disrupt it. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, what specific innovation milestone justified entry beyond price momentum or fear of missing out?
